req.title: Update patient (audit)
req.statement: A clinician shall update patient demographics; historical versions must be preserved. An AuditEvent must be emitted for every operation and tested end-to-end.

doc.reasoning: R-HLT-078 demands three verifiable behaviours: (1) a successful PUT by a clinician increments the resource version and preserves history; (2) a concurrent PUT with a stale If-Match ETag yields 409; (3) an AuditEvent is emitted for every mutating operation. I selected four tests covering the positive happy-path (EP/STC), the version-conflict error path (NEG/DT), the boundary of exactly one prior history entry after a single update (BVA), and the audit-event emission (STC). Each test creates its own patient via a POST fixture call, operates independently, and cleans up via a DELETE to remain idempotent.

tests.name: test_update_patient_demographics_success
preconditions: A patient resource exists (created by <create_patient> fixture)
actions: PUT /Patient/{id} with updated family name and correct If-Match ETag
Assert response body reflects new family name
trace: R-HLT-078 / Expected outcome: new version stored
tests.executable:
def test_update_patient_demographics_success(clinician_token, create_patient, fhir_base_url):
    patient_id, etag_v1 = create_patient
    headers = {
        "Authorization": f"Bearer {clinician_token}",
        "Content-Type": "application/fhir+json",
        "If-Match": etag_v1,
    }
    payload = <build_patient_payload>(patient_id, version="1", family="UpdatedFamily")
    resp = <http_client>.put(
        f"{fhir_base_url}/Patient/{patient_id}",
        json=payload,
        headers=headers,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["meta"]["versionId"] == "2"
    assert body["name"][0]["family"] == "UpdatedFamily"
    hist = <http_client>.get(
        f"{fhir_base_url}/Patient/{patient_id}/_history/1",
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {clinician_token}"},
    )
    assert hist.status_code == 200
    assert hist.json()["meta"]["versionId"] == "1"

tests.name: test_update_patient_version_conflict_returns_409
preconditions: A patient resource exists at version 1 (created by <create_patient> fixture)
A second clinician has already updated the patient to version 2 (via <advance_patient_version> helper)
actions: PUT /Patient/{id} supplying stale If-Match ETag pointing to version 1
trace: R-HLT-078 / Error pathway: 409 on version conflict
R-HLT-078 / Decision Table: stale ETag -> conflict
tests.executable:
def test_update_patient_version_conflict_returns_409(clinician_token, create_patient, fhir_base_url):
    patient_id, etag_v1 = create_patient
    <advance_patient_version>(patient_id, clinician_token, fhir_base_url)
    headers = {
        "Authorization": f"Bearer {clinician_token}",
        "Content-Type": "application/fhir+json",
        "If-Match": etag_v1,
    }
    payload = <build_patient_payload>(patient_id, version="1", family="ConflictFamily")
    resp = <http_client>.put(
        f"{fhir_base_url}/Patient/{patient_id}",
        json=payload,
        headers=headers,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 409
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("resourceType") == "OperationOutcome"
    assert body["issue"][0]["code"] == "conflict"

tests.name: test_history_contains_exactly_one_prior_version_after_single_update
preconditions: A patient resource exists at version 1 (created by <create_patient> fixture)
No prior updates have been made to this patient
actions: PUT /Patient/{id} once with valid If-Match ETag
Assert history bundle contains exactly 2 entries (version 1 and version 2)
trace: R-HLT-078 / BVA: boundary of 1 previous version after exactly 1 update
tests.executable:
def test_history_contains_exactly_one_prior_version_after_single_update(clinician_token, create_patient, fhir_base_url):
    patient_id, etag_v1 = create_patient
    auth = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {clinician_token}"}
    put_headers = {**auth, "Content-Type": "application/fhir+json", "If-Match": etag_v1}
    payload = <build_patient_payload>(patient_id, version="1", family="BoundaryFamily")
    put_resp = <http_client>.put(
        f"{fhir_base_url}/Patient/{patient_id}",
        json=payload,
        headers=put_headers,
    )
    assert put_resp.status_code == 200
    hist_resp = <http_client>.get(
        f"{fhir_base_url}/Patient/{patient_id}/_history",
        headers=auth,
    )
    assert hist_resp.status_code == 200
    bundle = hist_resp.json()
    entries = bundle.get("entry", [])
    assert len(entries) == 2
    versions = sorted([e["resource"]["meta"]["versionId"] for e in entries])
    assert versions == ["1", "2"]

tests.name: test_audit_event_emitted_after_patient_update
preconditions: A patient resource exists (created by <create_patient> fixture)
AuditEvent search endpoint is accessible to clinician or admin
actions: Assert at least one AuditEvent matches the update operation
trace: R-HLT-078 / Expected outcome: AuditEvent emitted for every operation
R-HLT-078 / STC: state transition clinician-authenticated -> resource-updated -> audit-recorded
tests.executable:
def test_audit_event_emitted_after_patient_update(clinician_token, create_patient, fhir_base_url):
    patient_id, etag_v1 = create_patient
    auth = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {clinician_token}"}
    put_headers = {**auth, "Content-Type": "application/fhir+json", "If-Match": etag_v1}
    payload = <build_patient_payload>(patient_id, version="1", family="AuditFamily")
    put_resp = <http_client>.put(
        f"{fhir_base_url}/Patient/{patient_id}",
        json=payload,
        headers=put_headers,
    )
    assert put_resp.status_code == 200
    audit_resp = <http_client>.get(
        f"{fhir_base_url}/AuditEvent",
        params={"entity": f"Patient/{patient_id}", "action": "U", "_sort": "-date", "_count": "1"},
        headers=auth,
    )
    assert audit_resp.status_code == 200
    bundle = audit_resp.json()
    assert bundle.get("total", 0) >= 1
    event = bundle["entry"][0]["resource"]
    assert event["action"] == "U"
    entity_refs = [e.get("what", {}).get("reference", "") for e in event.get("entity", [])]
    assert any(f"Patient/{patient_id}" in ref for ref in entity_refs)
